name: Herschel Evans
abstract: Herschel "Tex" Evans (9 March 1909 – 9 February 1939) was an American tenor saxophonist who was a member of the Count Basie Orchestra. He also worked with Lionel Hampton and Buck Clayton.

about_quotes.speaker: Ira Gitler
about_quotes.evidence: I remember him telling me, “Listen to the way Lester Young starts the band off on ‘Every Tub.’” ... like Herschel Evans and Lester Young on tenors
about_quotes.doc_title: Ira Gitler interviewed by Michael Woods, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Jerome
about_quotes.evidence: Herschel was a very good friend of mine, Herschel Evans, the tenor saxophone player with Basie.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Jerome interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Burrell
about_quotes.evidence: I really liked the way Coleman Hawkins and Lester Young…Herschel Evans, those kind of guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kenny Burrell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Count Basie
about_quotes.evidence: With Lester and Hershel starting off... they were the great combination as far as tenor goes... any recording date we ever had that anybody we had showed up... Chu Berry filled in

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: Basie’s band was built around Herschel and Lester... I remember we had a battle of music with Benny Goodman one time... we had Al Killian, myself, Buck Clayton and Ed Lewis.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Durham
about_quotes.evidence: Herschel Evans was in that band. We went and got him. You know, he was a cousin of mine... And it was three saxophones and Battles was on trumpet
